author: Nicolas Greliche
contents:
Written in Go, Hugo is an open source static site generator available under the Apache Licence 2.0. Hugo supports TOML, YAML and JSON data file types, Markdown and HTML content files and uses shortcodes to add rich content. Other notable features are taxonomies, multilingual mode, image processing, custom output formats, HTML/CSS/JS minification and support for Sass SCSS workflows.
Hugo makes use of a variety of open source projects including:
https://github.com/yuin/goldmark https://github.
title: crish

author: Nicolas Greliche
contents:
I believe that in the mind of most people, a fiction work that is purposely “educational” inevitably comes with an uninteresting - and probably childish - story. This rather strong association makes sense when we look at what exists. But I don’t think it has to be that way.
The start of the journey A few years ago, convinced that educational fiction was not incompatible with a good story, I decided to have a go at proving my point.
title: crish

author: Hugo Authors
contents: This article offers a sample of basic Markdown syntax that can be used in Hugo content files, also it shows whether basic HTML elements are decorated with CSS in a Hugo theme.
title: Markdown Syntax Guide

author: Hugo Authors
contents: Hugo ships with several Built-in Shortcodes for rich content, along with a Privacy Config and a set of Simple Shortcodes that enable static and no-JS versions of various social media embeds.
title: Rich Content

contents:
I think I’ve always had a knack for doing philosophy. This is much less true for art, where I’ve had to work very hard to get from drawing like this in my early 30s to being able to create what you can see here or here.
If you have a project requiring digital art and think I might be a good fit, feel free to connect.
title: Art

contents:
My scientific background makes me particularly interested in collaborating with other philosophers on experimental philosophy projects.
I have assisted many social science students and researchers in refining experimental designs similar to those typically used in experimental philosophy. Combined with my strong research methods expertise, this experience has given me a particularly sharp eye for good designs.
If you are planning an experimental philosophy project, I would love to be involved.
title: Collaboration

contents:

contents:
I have a dream that one day a blockbuster movie will invest a large portion of its budget in truly thoughtful educational content.
Until that happens, my comic crish — a semi-fantastic story filled with absurd humor and brief insights about statistics and the research world — is my humble attempt to show that entertaining stories can also convey deep philosophical and educational ideas.
The story of crish is ongoing, and in the next chapters, I plan to weave in some of the results of my current research.
title: Outreach

contents:
I am primarily interested in understanding how we can learn from data, and in exploring whether informal statistical reasoning can shed light on key questions in epistemology, decision theory and the philosophy of science.
My secondary research interests include contributions to the niche branch of the philosophy of comics and to the interdisciplinary field of experimental philosophy.
The research outputs resulting from my ongoing transition into these philosophical investigations are presented below.
title: Research
